The Vault v0.6.2 completes Open-Loops v2 Phase E with governed, installation-local canary enforcement.

Highlights:
- Adds authorized project lifecycle activation and rollback with optimistic concurrency, idempotency, reasons, and persisted evidence.
- Enforces active Work-project task admission inside the same database transaction as queue insertion, preventing caller or client bypasses.
- Preserves the Brain zero-loop invariant: Brain contexts reject delegated/normal work and legacy actionable `next_steps`, while explicit memory-maintenance tasks remain allowed.
- Keeps model-generated suggestions informational: enrichment output no longer silently creates persisted commitments or successor work.
- Exposes the governed lifecycle and task-admission contracts through Core, the MCP server, and Desktop Electron IPC/preload types.
- Refreshes vulnerable MCP transitive dependencies and pins patched sidecar versions; production dependency audits are clean at release verification time.

Rollout safety:
- No project names, user identities, machine paths, or installation-local rollout choices are embedded in source.
- Fresh installations begin with no classified or active project gates.
- Lifecycle activation remains an explicit, auditable installation-local action and can be rolled back.

Verification included the complete 353-test suite, workspace lint/type-check, Core and MCP declaration builds, a fresh-home MCP stdio handshake, Windows NSIS packaging, packaged-payload private-data scans, and independent correctness/security review.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
